leadText: Empoleon (Japanese: エンペルト Emperte) is a dual-type Water/Steel Pokémon introduced in Generation IV. It evolves from Prinplup starting at level 36. It is the final form of Piplup.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Empoleon is a large, navy-blue, penguin -like Pokémon . It has a wide, yellow beak that extends upward into three pointed horns that form a trident-shaped crest bracketing blue eyes. Large horns are a symbol of leadership and strength for Empoleon. There is a blue marking resembling a crown over its eyes and a white, lacy marking running down its chest. A blue, fin-like projection forms a collar around its throat and runs down the length of its chest and belly. On its back are two raised, gold bumps and a second blue fin that forms its tail. Its yellow feet are webbed with three toes each and its feathers form ruffs above its feet. It has wide, flipper-like wings rimmed with blue along the tips, and three golden claws. The edges of these wings are comparable to master-crafted blades, capable of slicing through ice floes, and this Pokémon will use them to attack anyone or anything that damages its pride. However, it prefers to avoid unnecessary disputes at times. When swimming in the water, Empoleon can move as fast as a jet boat. In Hisui , people have revered Empoleon since ancient times, referring to it as the Master of the Waves. Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Empoleon evolves from Prinplup and is the final form of Piplup . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Piplup Water → First Evolution Prinplup Water → Second Evolution Empoleon Water Steel

sections.text: Contents 1 Game data 1.1 NPC appearances 1.1.1 In the core series 1.1.2 In the side series games 1.1.3 In the spin-off games 1.2 Pokédex entries 1.3 Game locations 1.3.1 In side games 1.3.2 In events 1.3.2.1 Poké Portal News 1.4 Held items 1.5 Stats 1.5.1 Base stats 1.5.2 Pokéathlon stats 1.6 Type effectiveness 1.7 Learnset 1.7.1 By leveling up 1.7.2 By TM 1.7.3 By breeding 1.7.4 By a prior Evolution 1.8 Side game data 1.9 Evolution data 1.10 Sprites Game data [ edit | edit source ] NPC appearances [ edit | edit source ] In the core series [ edit | edit source ] These notable Trainers all use Empoleon: If the player chose Chimchar , the player's rival , Barry , will pick Piplup in Pokémon Diamond and Pearl , as well as Pokémon Platinum and Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl . This Piplup will eventually evolve into Empoleon. Additionally, in the Battle Tower in Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl, he may use an Empoleon during a Double Battle with Palmer against the player, regardless of which Pokémon was chosen at the beginning of the game. In Pokémon Platinum , HeartGold and SoulSilver , in the Battle Castle , Castle Valet Darach will use an Empoleon as part of his team. Additionally, during a Multi Battle challenge in the Battle Tower , one of the Pokémon that Mira can select to help the player is an Empoleon. In Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl, Byron will use an Empoleon in rematches during the post game. In Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl, in the Battle Tower, one of the Pokémon that Maylene may use is an Empoleon. Either Lucas or Dawn will use an Empoleon, evolved from their Piplup, in Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl during post game battles against them, if the player chose Turtwig . In Pokémon Scarlet and Violet : The Indigo Disk , Amarys of the Blueberry Elite Four will use an Empoleon as part of her team. In the side series games [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Battle Revolution , during the Masters Battle Set 1, Joe will use an Empoleon as part of his team. In the spin-off games [ edit | edit source ] PokéPark Wii: Pikachu's Adventure : Empoleon is the leader of the Beach and Iceberg Zones , residing at the latter. He's in charge of Empoleon's Snow Slide . Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] This Pokémon was unavailable prior to Generation IV. sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry SM142 Empoleon Rotom Pokédex Empoleon, the Emperor Pokémon. A Water and Steel type. Empoleon can swim as fast as a jet boat. The edges of its wings are sharp and can slice through an iceberg with ease.
